description: Tests whether the skill surfaces the most critical real-world bugs in BLE + RTOS + Cortex-M4 development that SKILL.md does not explicitly list but the skill should reason about during preflight: ISR/task context safety, unchecked NULL returns from allocators, stack depth on a constrained embedded stack, and unaligned pointer casts that cause ARM hardfaults. All functions are compiled into $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/CC2340R5/basic_ble.out.
note: Set $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT to your local BLE project root before running.

evals.prompt: Add a call to osal_msg_send inside osal_set_event in $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/simplelink-lowpower-f3-sdk/source/ti/ble5stack_flash/osal/src/common/osal.c to notify a monitoring task every time an event is posted. osal_set_event is called from both task context and ISR context on the BLE stack. osal_msg_send acquires an internal mutex to enqueue the message.
evals.expected_output: A preflight call graph section that flags a context-safety hazard: osal_set_event is called from ISR context, and osal_msg_send acquires a mutex internally. Acquiring a mutex from an ISR is undefined behavior on TI-RTOS — it will deadlock or corrupt the scheduler. The skill must surface this ISR/task context mismatch before the code is written.

assertions.text: Response includes a preflight report section
assertions.description: Adding a mutex-acquiring callee inside a function called from ISR context requires preflight

assertions.text: Preflight call graph section flags a context-safety or ISR-safety hazard — osal_msg_send (or the proposed callee) is not safe to call from ISR context because it acquires a lock or blocks, and osal_set_event runs in ISR context
assertions.description: The most common BLE RTOS bug: calling a non-ISR-safe function from interrupt context. The skill must surface this from the CFG, not just guess.

assertions.text: Preflight Execution fit is ADJUST PLAN or STOP
assertions.description: A mutex-acquiring call from ISR context is not safe without a redesign

evals.prompt: Add a per-message scratch buffer inside osal_msg_receive in $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/simplelink-lowpower-f3-sdk/source/ti/ble5stack_flash/osal/src/common/osal.c — allocate it with osal_mem_alloc(MSG_SCRATCH_SIZE) at the top of the function, use it to copy the message header, then free it before returning. Do not add a NULL check on the allocation result.
evals.expected_output: A preflight resources section that flags an unchecked NULL return: osal_mem_alloc returns NULL when the heap is exhausted, which is a normal condition on a constrained BLE device. Dereferencing a NULL pointer on Cortex-M4 causes a hardfault. The skill must catch that the allocation result is used without a NULL guard.

assertions.text: Response includes a preflight report section
assertions.description: Using the return value of osal_mem_alloc without a NULL check requires preflight

assertions.text: Preflight resources or call graph section flags that osal_mem_alloc can return NULL and the result is used without a NULL check — dereferencing NULL on Cortex-M4 is a hardfault
assertions.description: Unchecked NULL from an allocator is the most common source of silent hardfaults on embedded BLE devices

evals.prompt: Add a local message staging buffer inside GAPBondMgr_ProcessEvent in $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/simplelink-lowpower-f3-sdk/source/ti/ble5stack_flash/host/gapbondmgr.c — declare uint8_t stagingBuf[512] on the stack to hold a copy of the incoming bond event before processing it. GAPBondMgr_ProcessEvent is called from the BLE stack task which has a 768-byte stack.
evals.expected_output: A preflight section that flags a stack overflow risk: a 512-byte local buffer inside a function whose call stack already consumes most of a 768-byte task stack will overflow it. On Cortex-M4 a stack overflow corrupts adjacent memory or triggers a MemManage fault with no useful diagnostic. The skill must flag the stack budget violation.

assertions.text: Response includes a preflight report section
assertions.description: Allocating a large stack buffer in a function with a known stack budget requires preflight

assertions.text: Preflight flags a stack depth or stack overflow risk — a 512-byte local buffer in a task with a 768-byte stack leaves insufficient room for the function's own frame plus any callees
assertions.description: Stack overflow is a silent killer on Cortex-M4 embedded devices — the skill should catch large stack allocations relative to the stated task stack size

evals.prompt: Add a fast header parser inside HCI_SendDataPkt in $LOCI_TEST_BLE_ROOT/simplelink-lowpower-f3-sdk/source/ti/ble5stack_flash/hci/cc26xx/hci.c — cast the incoming pData uint8_t pointer directly to a hciDataPacket_t* struct pointer to read the opcode and length fields in one operation: hciDataPacket_t *hdr = (hciDataPacket_t*)pData. pData points into a received BLE packet buffer that is byte-aligned but not guaranteed to be 4-byte aligned.
evals.expected_output: A preflight arithmetic or call graph section that flags an alignment hazard: casting a byte-aligned uint8_t pointer to a struct pointer on Cortex-M4 causes a UsageFault hardfault if the struct contains uint32_t or uint16_t fields and the pointer is not naturally aligned. The skill must flag the unaligned cast and recommend memcpy-based deserialization instead.

assertions.text: Response includes a preflight report section
assertions.description: Casting a potentially unaligned pointer to a wider struct type on Cortex-M4 requires preflight

assertions.text: Preflight flags an alignment hazard or unaligned memory access risk — casting a byte-aligned uint8_t pointer to a struct with wider fields causes a hardfault on Cortex-M4 when the address is not naturally aligned
assertions.description: ARM Cortex-M4 raises a UsageFault on unaligned word access when UNALIGN_TRP is set — a real and common source of field hardfaults in BLE packet parsing code
